plugin.name: MW_Gauge
plugin.author: Proteles
plugin.purpose: Miniwindow spike demo: a live, timer-driven gauge

muclient.script:
local win = "mw_gauge_" .. GetPluginID() local WIDTH, HEIGHT = 260, 64 local tick = 0
local BG = 0x202020 local FRAME = 0x707070 local TRACK = 0x3A3A3A local FILL = 0x4080F0 -- orange-blue (BGR) bar local LABEL = 0xF0F0F0
function draw() tick = tick + 1 local fraction = (tick % 20) / 20 -- 0..1 sweep, ~20s period WindowRectOp(win, miniwin.rect_fill, 0, 0, 0, 0, BG) WindowRectOp(win, miniwin.rect_frame, 0, 0, 0, 0, FRAME)
-- Gauge track + proportional fill. local left, top, right, bottom = 12, 34, WIDTH - 12, 50 WindowRectOp(win, miniwin.rect_fill, left, top, right, bottom, TRACK) WindowRectOp(win, miniwin.rect_fill, left, top, left + (right - left) * fraction, bottom, FILL)
-- Centred label using WindowTextWidth (the layout-from-measurement pattern). local label = string.format("tick %d (%d%%)", tick, math.floor(fraction * 100)) local w = WindowTextWidth(win, "f", label) WindowText(win, "f", label, (WIDTH - w) / 2, 10, 0, 0, LABEL) WindowShow(win, true) end
function OnPluginInstall() WindowCreate(win, 0, 0, WIDTH, HEIGHT, miniwin.pos_top_center, 0, 0) WindowFont(win, "f", "Menlo", 12, false, false, false, false) draw() -- Redraw once a second (MUSHclient AddTimer: h, m, s). AddTimer("mw_gauge_tick", 0, 0, 1, "", timer_flag.Enabled, "draw()") end
